== Ablation Study

To demonstrate the effectiveness of our method, we conduct ablation experiments on panorama representation formats and network designs. For the ERP format, we directly fine-tune the base model to adapt to this panoramic representation. For the CP format, we train two models, CP-noloRA and CP-4DRoPE, separately to assess the impact of different cubeface-encodings. Technically, CP-loRA rearranges the six cube faces into a 2-row by 3-column rectangle in raster order, i.e., F, R, B on the first row, and D, L, U on the second row, while CP-4DRoPE assigns an independent 3D positional encoding to each cube face. As for the network designs, we compare our design of Pano-Perspective attention with two variants: "imaging quality", "motion smoothness", and "aesthetic quality". As shown in Fig. 3, the overlapping parts are eliminated (w/o Pano-Attn); or we eliminate the perspective-attention (w/o Pers-Attn). Additionally, we compare quantitative results.

The results of quantitative ablation are shown in Tab. 2, our full method outperforms the other alternative designs across four metrics. Since we conduct evaluations in a perspective manner, ERP-3D-LoRA performs best in "imaging quality". However, as shown in Fig. 6, though such perspective videos show good visual quality, there is still a discontinuity issue at the boundaries of the four faces. Therefore, it does not meet the broad usage of the high-quality panoramic video generation.

== User Study

Despite achieving advanced scores on Vbench [12], we conduct user studies, introducing subjective user ratings to further validate the superiority of our approach. We ask participants to vote on the tested videos based on four dimensions: "Spatial Continuity", "Temporal Quality", "Aesthetic Preference", and "Condition Alignment". "Spatial continuity" represents the coherence of scenes and structures in panoramic space, including their smoothness in 360°. "Aesthetic Preference" represents the projected videos evaluated by VBench [12]. This shows that although this method possesses strong dynamic ability, it lacks spatial consistency in panoramic space. "Condition Alignment" refers to the degree to which the generated panoramic video aligns with the input conditions, such as text or video.

#figure(image("figs/ablation.pdf", width: 75%), caption: [Ablation on different designs. ERP exhibits serious artifacts due to the natural gap in modality. Both Cube representation methods have spatial discontinuity issues. Without Perspective-Attention, it leads to misalignment with the input video. Our full method can generate reasonable and spatially consistent results.])

Finally, we collect 50 valid questionnaires, each containing 14 sets of videos for comparison, and the results are shown in Fig. 7. Overall, Imagine360 [25] is the second choice among participants, while 360DVD [31] and Follow-Your-Canvas [5], receive the fewest votes. Our approach receives the highest number of votes across all four dimensions, surpassing 360DVD [31], Follow-Your-Canvas [5], and Imagine360 [25], demonstrating our method's ability to generate satisfactory panoramic results.

= Conclusion

In this work, we present ViewPoint, a novel framework for representing and generating panoramic videos leveraging modern generative diffusion models. Specifically, we design a novel representation distinct from traditional panoramic image and video representations, which has the advantages of both spatial continuity and temporal consistency. Through our proposed Pano-Perspective attention mechanism, our trained model preserves the global spatial-temporal continuity of the generated panoramic videos. With our proposed Pano-Perspective attention mechanism, our trained model perceives the global panoramic space, effectively leveraging the strong priors of pretrained perspective video generation models. Extensive results, both qualitative and quantitative, show that our approach outperforms previous methods. We look forward to the effectiveness of our method, and we believe ViewPoint can and Imagine360 [25], demonstrating our method's ability to generate satisfactory panoramic results.
